Why U-Net works
Contracting path: capture context and semantics.
Skip connections: pass high-resolution detail forward.
Expanding path: recover pixel-level localization.
Typical use
Especially effective when boundaries matter and training data is limited, including biomedical imaging.

Three Ways to Up-sample
18
Method
How it works
Learnable
Typical Effect
Main Issue
Nearest
Copy pixels
No
Blocky edges
Staircase artifacts
Bilinear
Interpolate neighbors
No
Smooth edges
Blur boundaries
Transposed Conv
Insert zeros + convolve
Yes
Sharp details
Checkerboard artifacts
Goal: Recover spatial resolution after down-sampling
Input: H/2 × W/2 × C → Output: H × W × C
Fixed interpolation methods restore size.
Transposed convolution learns how to restore structure.
Nearest
Copies pixels directly. Fast and simple, but edges can look blocky.
Bilinear
Interpolates neighboring values. Smoother than nearest, but may blur boundaries.
Transposed conv
Inserts zeros and learns how to convolve. Sharp and flexible, but can create checkerboard artifacts.

In modern architectures, interpolation + convolution is often preferred over pure transposed convolution.

Interpolation-Based
Upsampling
23
Each new pixel is a weighted average of the four surrounding pixels.
Nearest neighbor repeats nearby values and keeps edges crisp but blocky.
Bilinear uses a weighted combination of neighbors and smooths the result.
Bicubic uses a larger neighborhood and can produce smoother curves.

In transposed convolution,
each input pixel contributes to
multiple output locations
.
When those contributions overlap,
we
sum them

Each input pixel generates a weighted footprint. Overlapping footprints are summed.

Simple Example Transposed Convolution (Stride = 1)
Each input pixel writes a weighted footprint into the output.
When footprints overlap, their contributions are summed.
This is why transposed convolution can both enlarge and learn structure.

Transposed Convolution
No padding, stride = 2, Kernel = 3x3
39
Worked Example: Stride-2 Transposed Convolution
Insert zeros according to the stride.
Apply a standard convolutional kernel on the expanded grid.
Sum the overlapping responses to form the final output.
Practical implication
Kernel size, stride, and padding jointly determine the final output size and the overlap pattern.

possible_slide_title: Limitations of Transposed Convolution: Checkerboard Artifacts
visible_text:
Limitations of Transposed Convolution: Checkerboard Artifacts
https://
towardsdatascience.com
/transposed-convolution-demystified-84ca81b4baba
Chequered
board Artifacts
40
Uneven overlap can create periodic texture patterns in the output.
The problem is most visible when stride and kernel size interact poorly.
A reliable mitigation is interpolation or resize followed by a regular convolution.

Summary
41
Transposed Convolution (Core Idea)
Zero insertion
Standard convolution
Overlapping summation
Matrix interpretation: transpose of convolution operator.
Practical Insight
Interpolation + Conv is often stable and effective
Transposed Conv may cause checkerboard artifacts
Design choice depends on task and architecture
Segmentation is structured prediction: preserve spatial reasoning while learning semantic meaning.
